comment.body: Hey guys can you please tag me where Alma explained the spot/vol beta, I need to study that again.
comment.raw_body: Hey guys can you please tag me where Alma explained the spot/vol beta, I need to study that again.

comment.body: "beta meassures the correlation. Spot/vol beta means how muchthe spot and the volatility, atm vol, fix strike vol moves together. when it isnegative, they move opposite. when it is positive, they move together. ofcourse, it has a spectrum, not black&white. the "normal" versionis the negative (short) spot/vol beta, when spot up/vol down. why? bcs it hiscase, investors are long stock, and use hedges to hedge the downside by buyingOTM puts and selling OTM calls, buying the skew. (bcs skew is 25d put minus 25dcall) by doing this, they put market makers (who take the opposite of thetrade: long OTM calls, short OTM puts, selling the skew) into long vanna: so asspot moves up, they become longer vega that they hedge by selling vol. Thisreflexively dumps volatility down if market moves up. But if market moves down,MMs become shorter vega, means they have to buy vol. reflexively increasingvolatility. and not just that... gamma: they are longer upside gamma, andshorter downside gamma (positive speed). So, volatility compresses to theupside (as more trading against the price actions), and expands to thedownside, resulting: NEGATIVE SPOT/VOL BETA. but if the opposite happens, maniaon the options market, and right-tail hedging: investors buying OTM calls andselling OTM puts, selling the skew. this puts market makers into long skew, andshort vanna, means: as spot moves up, they become shorter vega, so they need tobuy vega. Also shorter upside gamma, and longer downside gamma (negative speed)So vol expands if market moves up, createing right-tail risk, low liquidity tothe upside due to this one-sided crowded trade. And compresses to the downside,as there where liquidity stands. This results POSITIVE SPOT/VOL BETA if the bideffects the tails as well, tail OTM puts (that are synthetic VIX calls),bidding also vol of vol, you will see positive spot/vix beta. thats whenblow-off top happens."

(bcs skew is 25d put minus 25dcall) by doing this, they put market makers (who take the opposite of thetrade: long OTM calls, short OTM puts, selling the skew) into long vanna: so asspot moves up, they become longer vega that they hedge by selling vol. Thisreflexively dumps volatility down if market moves up. But if market moves down,MMs become shorter vega, means they have to buy vol. reflexively increasingvolatility. and not just that... gamma: they are longer upside gamma, andshorter downside gamma (positive speed). So, volatility compresses to theupside (as more trading against the price actions), and expands to thedownside, resulting: NEGATIVE SPOT/VOL BETA. but if the opposite happens, maniaon the options market, and right-tail hedging: investors buying OTM calls andselling OTM puts, selling the skew. this puts market makers into long skew, andshort vanna, means: as spot moves up, they become shorter vega, so they need tobuy vega. Also shorter upside gamma, and longer downside gamma (negative speed)So vol expands if market moves up, createing right-tail risk, low liquidity tothe upside due to this one-sided crowded trade. And compresses to the downside,as there where liquidity stands. This results POSITIVE SPOT/VOL BETA if the bideffects the tails as well, tail OTM puts (that are synthetic VIX calls),bidding also vol of vol, you will see positive spot/vix beta. thats whenblow-off top happens."

comment.body:
She is holding March expo puts. If we V shape recover into March OPEX then that’s a waste of cash.

I thought she was thinking that we see a subtle decline lower with potentially violent rallies like Friday, chopping in a “bull flag”/triangle like  pattern into March OPEX… only instead of breaking out to the upside (as is usually the case with a bull flag), we breakout to the downside in an elevator like capitulation decline.

She has stated this environment would be great for keeping put option premiums high while allowing them to decay so bears don’t make money.

And since the call side is underpriced, there will be more opportunity for “arbitrage” by taking advantage of those violent rallies.

But given this I don’t know why she would be holding long dated March Expo puts if they will be prone to decay and expiring worthless. I would assume spot is getting close to where she bought them last Wednesday.
